# Toy Pitch Detection

## Lesson Goal

Estimate pitch from the strongest FFT peak.

## What to Notice

The method is intentionally fragile, which makes it useful for teaching model limitations.

## Work With the Notebook

- [Open the notebook source](https://github.com/buildLittleWorlds/marimo-audio-self-tutorial/blob/main/notebooks/08_detect_pitch_fft/notebook.py)
- [Open the editable browser notebook](https://buildlittleworlds.github.io/marimo-audio-self-tutorial/notebooks/08_detect_pitch_fft/)

Run locally:

```bash
uv run marimo edit notebooks/08_detect_pitch_fft/notebook.py
```

## Feedback Lens

A strong response names when this method works and when it fails.

## Instructor Notes

Use this lesson as a small checkpoint. Before asking students to add features,
ask them to name the input, the transformation, the output, and the current
limitation. That habit will matter more in later transcription notebooks than
any one line of code.
